title: CheXphoto Chest X-Ray Photo Robustness Dataset

data_urls.access_notes: AIMI page links the dataset through Stanford Redivis; account/terms may be required.
Use for stable citation and source verification.
Use for benchmark semantics and transform definitions.

instructions: Use the Stanford AIMI/Redivis source rather than unofficial mirrors.
Record patient ID, source CheXpert split, original CXR ID, transform type, natural-versus-synthetic flag, pathology labels, and split.
Start with validation/test metadata or a sampled transform subset before pulling all images.

scale.download_size: 10,000+ transformed chest X-ray photos from CheXpert-derived images; account-gated portal likely

recommended_axes.name: acquisition_transform_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of test images to training images by natural-photo, synthetic-photo, angle, glare, crop, blur, and display-artifact transforms.

recommended_axes.name: source_xray_support
recommended_axes.definition: Whether the same or similar original radiograph appears in training under a different transform.

recommended_axes.name: pathology_label_support
recommended_axes.definition: Class prevalence and co-occurrence support for each pathology label under transform shift.

useful_for: Medical-imaging examples where the generalization axis is acquisition/process shift rather than anatomy alone.
Testing whether a CXR model is robust to photos of radiographs and synthetic display artifacts.
Demonstrating leakage-aware split construction over patient, original image, and transform identity.
leakage_risks: The same original radiograph can appear under multiple transforms.
Patient overlap with CheXpert-derived training data can invalidate prospective claims.
Transform labels can become shortcuts if train/test split does not separate acquisition conditions cleanly.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified CheXphoto/CheXternal as CXR generalization resources; official AIMI page verifies Redivis access, DOI, and dataset scale.
